paragraphs.text: Since we are going to start learning to pray, I would like to leave Of course, what I understand by 'learning to pray' is not an attempt to justifying or explaining this on a purely speculative level. Rather, I I would like to point out what one must be aware of and what one can do if they wish to pray. As a beginner that I myself am, I will assume that you are also a beginner, and we will try to start together. I am not addressing anyone who aspires to a degree of mystical prayer or higher state of perfection, because of those there are things that should not be learned. When God comes to us or we reach God in certain exceptional circumstances, because things suddenly appear to us with a dimension que nunca habíamos percibido en ellas, o de pronto descubrimos en the deepest part of us A place where prayer resides and of There it sprouts, there is no problem with the prayer. When we have awareness of the existence of God, we feel in his presence, him we love, we talk to him/her.

Initially, there is, therefore, a very important problem: the situation of the one for whom God seems to be absent. This is what I I would like to talk now. It is obvious that I am not referring to a real absence. -- God is never really absent --, but from the 'feeling' of the absence we have. We stand before God and shout before an empty sky, from which we receive no reply. We turn in all addresses and we couldn't find him. What should we think about this situation?

First of all, it is very important to remember that the sentence is a I find a relationship, a relationship that is deep, and this. relationship cannot be forced either in us or in God. The fact that may God make Himself present or leave us with the feeling of His absence is part of this living and real relationship. If we could mechanically attract him to a date, force him to meet us, simply because we have chosen this moment to 5

meeting with Him, there would be no relationship or encounter. We can do that with an image, with imagination or with the various idols that we can place before us instead of God; we cannot do none of that with the living God, as we cannot do it with a living person. A relationship should start and develop in mutual freedom. If one looks at the relationship in terms of 'mutual' relationship, one You will see that God can complain about us much more than we complain about Him that He does not reveal Himself to us present during the few minutes we reserved for him; but what to say from the twenty-three and a half hours during which God may be knocking on our door and we answered: 'I'm busy, I "I feel", or when we do not respond at all, because not even we hear the call at the door of our heart, of our mind, our consciousness, of our life. So there are occasions when the that we do not have the right to complain about the absence of God, because we are much more absent than he ever is.

The second important thing is a face-to-face encounter with God. it is always a reason for judgment for us. We cannot find to God in prayer, meditation, or contemplation without being saved or condemned. I don't want to say this in the final terms of eternal condemnation or eternal salvation given and received already, but it is always a critical moment, a crisis. 'Crisis' comes from the Greek and it means judgment. Finding God face to face in prayer is a critical moment in our lives, and thanks be to Him for not always present ourselves when we wish to, because we could unable to bear this encounter. Remember the many passages from the Holy Scriptures that tell us how bad it is to meet face to face with God, because God is power, God is truth, God is purity. Therefore, the first thought that we must have when we do not tangibly perceive the divine presence is a thought of gratitude. God is merciful; not it comes at an inappropriate time. It gives us the opportunity to judge ourselves we, to understand, and not to appear before Him, in His presence, when it could mean condemnation.

I would like to give an example of this. Many years ago, a came a A man came to see me. He asked me to show him God. I replied that no. I could, but I added that even if I could, I wouldn't be able to see it because I thought -- and I think -- that to find God one must have something. in common with Him, something that gives you eyes to see, perceptiveness to 6

to perceive. Then he asked me why I thought that way, and I I suggested that he think for a moment and tell me if there was any passage in the Gospel that will particularly move him, to see which one it was the connection between him and God. He said: "Yes, in the eighth chapter of the Gospel according to Saint John, the passage that deals with the adulterous woman.

I said to him: 'Well, this is one of the most beautiful passages and of the more emotional. Now sit down and ask yourself who you are in the described scene. Are you the Lord, or at least are you in His presence? side, full of mercy, understanding, and full of faith in that a woman who can repent and become a new creature? Is it Are you the woman caught in adultery? Are you one of the men? old people who leave quickly because they realize their own sins, or one of the young people who stay?" Thought by a few minutes and then he said: 'No, I think I'm the only Jew who does not he left, but he would have stoned the woman." I said: "Thanks to God that He has not allowed him to see Him face to face.

This may be an extreme example, but how often Do we stumble upon similar situations ourselves? No, because we flatly reject the word of God or the example of God; but, though in a less violent way, we do what the soldiers do they did during the Passion. We would like to cover Christ's eyes to to be able to hit freely without being seen. Don't we do this, in certain way, when we ignore the divine presence and act from agreement with our wishes, our disposition, contrary to everything What is the will of God? We try to blind you, but, in reality, we blind ourselves. In those moments, how can we go to their presence? We can do it, truly, repentant, remorseful, but we cannot go as if we wished to be received immediately: with love and friendship.

Read several passages from the Gospel. People much more important than we hesitated before receiving Christ. Remember the centurion who he asked Christ to heal his servant. Christ said, 'I will go,' but the The centurion said: 'No. Just say a word and he will be healed.' Shall we do it? us this?

Do we turn to God and say: "Do not make Yourself tangible, perceptibly present before me. It is enough that you say a word and I will be healed. It is enough that you say a word and things will 'happen'.
I don't need more for the moment? Or the case of Peter in his boat, 7

after the miraculous catch of fish, when he fell to his knees and said: "Depart from me, Lord, for I am a poor sinner." He asked the Lord. that he would leave his boat because he felt humble, and he felt humble because, suddenly, he perceived the greatness of Jesus. Do we Have we ever done this? When we read the Gospels and the image of Christ it appears impressive, glorious, when we pray and give ourselves account for the greatness, the holiness of God, we sometimes say: "No Do I deserve for Him to come to my side? Not to mention all the occasions when we should be aware that He cannot come to us because we are not there to receive you.

We want something from Him, not Him at all. Is that a relationship? Do we Do we behave that way with our friends? Do we seek what Is friendship something we give, or is it the friend whom we love? Is this the case? regarding the Lord?

Let's think about our prayers, yours and mine; think about the warmth, the depth and intensity of their prayers when they refer to someone they love or something that matters a lot to them in their life. In that case, your heart is open, your entire inner being is Taken in prayer. Does that mean that God cares about you?

No, it does not mean that. It simply means that the object of your Sentence matters to you. Because when you have made your passionate, deep and intense prayer related to the person you love or the situation that troubles you and you move on to the next topic, which does not Does it matter so much if, suddenly, you feel indifferent, what has changed?

Has God cooled off? Has He left? No, it means that all the joy, all the intensity of his prayer did not arise from the presence of God, or of his faith in Him or his desire for Him or to be aware of her presence; was born solely from her interest in the person, he or she, or of the thing, not by God. How can we to feel surprised, then, that this absence of God affects us?
It is us who are absent, it is us who we cool down the moment we are not interested in God.
Why? Because he doesn't matter that much.

There are other ways of God's ‘ absence’. If we are authentic, if we are truly ourselves, God can be present and can do something with us. But at the moment we try to be what we are not, there is nothing left to do or to say; we become a fictional personality, a presence unreal, and to that unreal presence God cannot approach. 8

In order to pray, we must be within the context defined as Kingdom of God. We must acknowledge that He is God, that He is King, we must surrender to Him. We must at least feel worried about their will, even if we are not yet able to fulfill it. But if it is not so, if we treat God like the rich young man who he could not follow Christ because he was too rich, so how Can we find him? Often what we want to find by through the prayer, through the deep relationship with God, What we long for is simply another period of happiness; we are not ready to sell everything we have to buy the pearl of Great price. So how are we going to get this great gem? price? Is that what we expect to achieve? Isn't it the same in the human relationships when a man or a woman is They fall in love, does the rest of the people not care in the same way?
To put it in a brief formula from antiquity: 'When a man He has a girlfriend, he is not surrounded by men and women, but by people.

Isn't that what could, what should happen regarding all our riches when we turn to God? Surely it they would return pale and gray, precisely a dull background against which the only figure that matters would appear with intense relief. We I would just like a touch of celestial blue in the overall picture of our life, in which there are so many dark corners. God is ready to be out of it, He is ready to carry it complete like a cross, but not ready to be part simply of our life.

So when we think about the absence of God, is it not worth it wondering who to blame? We always accuse God, directly to Him or in front of other people, of being absent, of not to be there when needed, nor to respond when called. A sometimes we are more "pious" (much, in quotes) and we say piously: 'God is testing my patience, my faith, my humility." We find all sorts of ways to turn judgments around. of God upon us in the form of praises to ourselves.
We are so patient that we can even endure God!

Isn't it true? When I was a young priest, I preached a sermon, one of the many that I preached in a parish, and a young woman came And he said to me: 'Father Anthony, you must be the devil.' I replied: 9

"It's true that I'm bad, but how do you know?" She said: "Because it has you described our sins so well, that you must have them committed all!" Of course, the description of the evil ones thoughts and vile attitudes that I am having now it is probably mine and not yours; but it may also belong to you, although small.

What we must begin with, if we wish to pray, is with the certainty that we are sinners and need salvation, that we are separated from God and that we cannot live without Him, and that everything what we can offer to God is our desperate anxiety for to be such that He receives us, receives us in repentance, receive us with mercy and love. And thus, from the beginning, prayer is truly our humble ascension towards God, a moment when we turn to God, hesitant to draw near, knowing that if we meet Him too soon, before That His grace may have had time to help us find ourselves.

A trial will be held with him. And all we can do is to address Him with all reverence, all veneration, worship supreme, the fear of God that we are capable, with all the attention and seriousness that we can have, and to ask him to do something with us that allows us to meet face to face, not for a judgment, not for condemnation, but for eternal life.

I would like to remind you of the parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector. The the publican enters and stays at the back of the temple. He knows that he is convicted; knows that, in terms of justice, there is no hope for he because he is an intruder in the Kingdom of God, the kingdom of righteousness or the kingdom of love, because it does not belong to the kingdom of righteousness nor to the kingdom of love. But in the cruel, violent, horrible life that he leads has learned something that the righteous Pharisee has no idea about. It has learned that in a world of rivalry, in a world of animals predators, in a world of cruelty and lack of heart, only do one can have hope in an act of mercy, an act of compassion, a completely unexpected act that is not due to anything must not relate to natural relations, which will stop cruel acts, violent, heartless of the world we live in. All it knows, for example, for being himself an extortionist, a moneylender, a thief, etc., it is that there are moments when without reason, because it does not form part of the world's attitude, he will forgive a debt because of suddenly his heart has softened; that on another occasion he will not take 10

someone to prison because a face may have reminded him of something or a Your voice must have reached the heart. There is no logic in this. It does not form part of the world's attitude, it is not the style in which one normally is it behaves. It's something that arises from within, that is completely absurd to what one cannot resist; and he knows also, probably, how often he himself has been saved from the final catastrophe by the intrusion of the unexpected and the impossible; mercy, compassion, forgiveness. That is why it remains at the back of the temple, Knowing that the entire kingdom within the temple is the kingdom of righteousness and of divine love, to which it does not belong and within which it cannot to enter. But he also knows from experience that the impossible happens, and that is why it says: "Have mercy, break the rules of the righteousness, breaks the rules of religion, descends with mercy to we, who do not have the right to be forgiven or admitted." And I think we should continually start from there.

You will probably remember the two passages of Saint Paul where He says: 'My power is manifested in weakness.' Weakness is not the kind of weakness that we show by sinning and forgetting about God, but rather the class of weakness that means being completely light, completely transparent, completely abandoned in the hands of God. Generally, we try to be strong, and from that In this way, we prevent God from manifesting His power.

Do you remember how you were taught to write when you were a child? Your mother put a pencil in her hand, took hers with one hand and she started to move it. Since you didn't know what she intended to do, he let his hand completely free in hers. This is what I want to speak of the power of God manifesting in weakness.
You can also think of this in relation to a candle.

A sail can catch the wind and be used to maneuver a boat only because of how light it is. If instead of a candle you were to place a solid table would not work; it is the weakness of the candle that makes it. sensible to the wind. The same happens with the gauntlet and the glove of surgeon. How strong the gauntlet is and how delicate the glove is, and, without embargo, in intelligent hands can perform miracles by itself Delicacy! In the same way, one of the things that God continues teaching us is to replace the imaginary and insignificant amount of disturbing strength that we have for that weakness of surrender, del abandono en las manos de Dios. Puedo darles un ejemplo de ello. 11

Twenty-five years ago, a friend of mine, who had two children, was killed. in the liberation of Paris. His children had always hated me because they felt jealous that their father had a friend; but when he father died they turned to me because I had been a friend of their father.

One of his children was a fifteen-year-old girl, who came one day to come to my clinic (I was a doctor before becoming a priest) and saw that, in addition to my instruments, I had a volume of the Gospels on my table. So, with the conviction that characterizes youth said: "I cannot understand how a man who is supposed educated people can believe such stupid things." I said to him: "Have you read?" She said: "No." Then I responded: "Remember that only the most stupid people pass judgment on things they do not know." After that, she read the Gospels and became interested so much that it changed his whole life because he began to pray. God gave him a proof of her presence and she lived off that fact for a time. Then she fell ill with an incurable disease, and wrote me a letter when I had already been ordained a priest and was in England, and I said: 'Since my body has begun to weaken and has begun to decline, my spirit has been revitalized more than ever and I perceive the divine presence easily and joyfully." I replied: "Do not trust in may it last. When you have lost a little more strength, do not you will feel ready to turn to God and then you will feel that you do not you have access to Him." After a certain time, he wrote to me from new, and said: "Yes, I have become so weak now that I cannot do the effort to direct my spirit towards God, not even to desire it actively, so God has disappeared." But I said to him: "Now do something else. Try to learn humility in its true sense from this word.

The word "humility" comes from the Latin "humus", which means fertile land. For me, humility is not what is often we understand: the false lamb-like docility, trying to imagine that we are the worst in the world and trying to convince others that our A false way of acting indicates that we are convinced of it. The humility is the situation of the earth. The earth is always there, always without being paid attention to, never remembered, always stepped on by everyone, something discarded where we throw the waste, what we don't need. It's there, silent and accepting. everything and, miraculously, bringing forth new from all the waste wealth despite corruption, transforming the corruption itself in life potential and in new possibilities of creation, open to 12

soil, open to the rain, ready to receive any seed that let's sow and be able to give thirty for one, the sixth, the hundred. of each seed. I told that woman: "Learn to be that before of God; abandon yourself, surrender, prepare to receive anything from the people and anything from God." Indeed, he received a lot of the people; after six months, her husband, tired of having a dying woman, they abandoned her; thus they threw waste on her abundance, but God also made His light shine and made it fall rain, because after a brief pause she wrote to me again and She said: 'I am completely finished. I cannot turn to God,' but it is God who descends towards me.

This is not only to illustrate what I said, but something fundamental: it is in weakness that God manifests His power and it is this that situation where the absence of God can become presence of God. We cannot capture God. But when we stay, already like the tax collector, as this young woman is, apart from the Kingdom of the "rights", only in the Kingdom of mercy can we find God.

Try to think about the absence of God and understand that before that can knock on the door -- and remember that it is not just at the door of the Kingdom understood in a general sense, but Christ It really says "I am the door)) --, before you knock on the door, You must be aware that you are out. If you waste time imagining that you are already within the kingdom of God, it makes no sense to knock on any door to have it open. It is obvious, you will look around seeking the angels and the saints and where is the mansion what belongs to him, and when he sees nothing but darkness and walls, he can it is legitimately surprising that Paradise is so little attractive. We must all realize that we are not yet in he, who we are still strangers in the kingdom of God, and ask ourselves: Where is the door and what is it called?
In the following chapter, we will explore this topic in depth. knock on the door and with the intention of entering, to be an inhabitant of paradise, the place where prayer is possible. 13
